REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ADDRESS ALL ISSUES RAISED BY DEMURRER IN GOOD FAITH AND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM TO THE PROPERTY AND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. ALL PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR, CRC AND CCP IN FUTURE. FAILURE TO COMPLY WILL NOT BE TOLERATED IN FUTURE AND MAY RESULT IN MOTIONS BEING TAKEN OFF CALENDAR OR DENIED. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. WHILE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FOLLOW THE PROPER PROCEDURE TO SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND PRIOR TO FILING AN AMENDED COMPLAINT (WITH NEW CAUSES OF ACTION), STRIKING WELL-PLEADED CAUSES OF ACTION SERVES NO LEGITIMATE PURPOSE. THE COURT NOTES PLAINTIFF'S DISREGARD OF THE RULES AND WILL NOT TOLERATE ANY VIOLATIONS OF THE CCP, CRC OR LOCAL RULES IN THE FUTURE. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE BASIS FOR HIS EQUITABLE CLAIM TO TITLE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE TERMS OF THE ORAL CONTRACT; THE PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT; FACTS ADDRESSING THE ISSUE OF STATUTE OF FRAUDS; ANY FACTS ADDRESSING THE ISSUE OF STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS (ACCRUAL OR DELAYED DISCOVERY). PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO ALLEGE FACTS REGARDING PRESENT ACTUAL CONTROVERSY. ALL AMENDMENTS SHALL BE IN BOLD FONT. THE COURT NOTES THAT PARTIES AGAIN CHOSE NOT TO COMPLY WITH SFLR. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT; OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF OPPOSITION. BOTH PARTIES HAVE BEEN WARNED THAT COMPLIANCE IS MANDATORY AND ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH RULES ON SEPTEMBER 12, 2017. ANY NON-COMPLIANCE IN FUTURE WILL RESULT IN MOTIONS BEING TAKEN OFF CALENDAR OR GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER OF DEFENDANTS ALI MOINI NAZERI AND AKBAR MOINI NAZERI TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 62323666) FILED BY DEFENDANT NAZERI, ALI MOINI NAZERI, AKBAR MOINI

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION: 1) CAUSES OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE AND PARTITION. PLAINTIFF HAS NO CURRENT LEGAL INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY AND THEREFORE LACKS STANDING. IN ORDER TO PROCEED WITH THE ACTION PLAINTIFF SHALL ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS TO UNWIND/CANCEL/VOID THE FOLLOWING TWO TRANSFERS: (1) OCOTBER 19, 2010 FORM SHAFE TO AKBAR AND (2) OCTOBER 5, 2010 FROM REZA TO SHAFE. ADDITIONALLY SHAFE TO BE NAMED AS A PARTY TO THE ACTION (AS PLAINTIFF OR DEFENDANT) BECAUSE SHE IS A NECESSARY PARTY DUE TO HER (PAST) INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY. REZA TRANSFERRED TITLE TO SHAFE PER HER REQUEST SO THAT "SHE HAD AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST.". REZA AND AKBAR AGREED THAT THEY WOULD STILL OWN THE PROPERTY 50/50 NOTWITHSTANDING TRANSFER OF 100% INTEREST TO SHAFE ON OCTOBER 5, 2010. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT SHAFE WAS A PART OF THIS AGREEMENT OR THAT SHE WAS EVEN ON NOTICE OF THE EXISTENCE OF SUCH AGREEMENT MADE IN RELATION WITH TRANSFER TO SHAFE. ON OCTOBER 19, 2010 SHAFE, AS THE SOLE TITLE OWNER, TRANSFERRED TITLE TO AKBAR. REZA ALLEGES THAT THIS TRANSFER WAS OBTAINED BY FORCE AND UNDUE INFLUENCE, BUT FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS STANDING TO BRING SUCH A CLAIM. 2) DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION: (A) DECLARATION REGARDING (I) TITLE AND (II) PARTITION IS DUPLICATIVE OF OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE AND PARTITION AND CANNOT BE MAINTAINED FOR THE SAME REASONS STATED ABOVE; (B) DECLARATION REGARDING MORTGAGE - PLAINTIFF HAS TO ALLEGE ACTUAL CONTROVERSY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Downing, Esq. of Downing Law Offices, 408-564-7020, appearing for Reza Nazeri, Plaintiff and moving party.

Alex Sears, Esq. 925-255-5442, appearing for Ali Nazeri, Defendant and opposing party.

ARGUED.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. While Plaintiff failed to follow the proper procedure to seek leave to amend prior to filing an amended complaint (with new causes of action), striking well-pleaded causes of action serves no legitimate purpose. The Court notes Plaintiff's disregard of the rules and will not tolerate any violations of the CCP, CRC or Local Rules in the future. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Downing, Esq. of Downing Law Offices, 408-564-7020, appearing for Reza Nazeri, Plaintiff and moving party.

Alex Sears, Esq. 925-255-5442, appearing for Ali Nazeri, Defendant and opposing party.

ARGUED. Mr. Sears stated that he contested the tentative ruling. The Court replied that there are two new causes of action and the mother has been added as party to the case. Mr. Sears requested clarification on the statue of limitations. The Court stated that what before it today are the demurrers. Mr. Downing argued that that he sufficiently alleged the contract, that it is time to sell the property and distribute the proceeds.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. All causes of action asserted in the complaint are sufficiently alleged. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend all three causes of action:
1) Causes of Action for Quiet Title and Partition. Plaintiff has no current legal interest in the Property and therefore lacks standing. In order to proceed with the action Plaintiff shall allege in good faith facts to unwind/cancel/void the following two transfers: (1) Ocotber 19, 2010 form Shafe to Akbar and (2) October 5, 2010 from Reza to Shafe. Additionally Shafe to be named as a party to the action (as Plaintiff or Defendant) because she is a necessary party due to her (past) interest in the Property. Reza transferred title to Shafe per her request so that "she had an ownership interest.". Reza and Akbar agreed that they would still own the property 50/50 notwithstanding transfer of 100% interest to Shafe on October 5, 2010. There is no allegation that Shafe was a part of this agreement or that she was even on notice of the existence of such agreement made in relation with transfer to Shafe. On October 19, 2010 Shafe, as the sole title owner, transferred title to Akbar. Reza alleges that this transfer was obtained by force and undue influence, but fails to allege facts to support his standing to bring such a claim.

2) Declaratory Relief Cause of action: (a) declaration regarding (i) title and (ii) partition is duplicative of other causes of action for Quiet Title and Partition and cannot be maintained for the same reasons stated above; (b) declaration regarding mortgage - Plaintiff has to allege actual controversy.

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege basis for his equitable claim to title, including but not limited to, the terms of the oral contract; the parties to the contract; facts addressing the issue of Statute of Frauds; any facts addressing the issue of Statute of Limitations (accrual or delayed discovery). Plaintiff must also allege facts regarding present actual controversy. All amendments shall be in bold font. The Court notes that parties again chose not to comply with SFLR. Moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the operative complaint; opposing party failed to provide a courtesy copy of opposition. Both parties have been warned that compliance is mandatory and ordered to comply with rules on September 12, 2017. Any non-compliance in future will result in motions being taken off calendar or granted as unopposed. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/AJR)
